Central Assistance Committed ₹ 178233 Cr. Central Assistance Released ₹ 8.77 Lakh Cr. Total Investment Public Notice (Click to View) | Expression of Interest (EoI) for shortlisting of Entities for Construction, Operation and Maintenance of Rental Housing by Private/Public Entities for urban poor, working women, employees of Industries, Industrial Estates, Institutions and other eligible EWS/LIG families under Model-2 of ARH vertical of PMAY-U 2.0 | National Progress | State Wise Progress | City Wise Progress | Affordable Rental Housing Complexes (ARHCs) Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs has initiated Affordable Rental Housing Complexes (ARHCs), a sub-scheme under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U). This will provide ease of living to urban migrants/ poor in Industrial Sector as well as in non-formal urban economy to get access to dignified affordable rental housing close to their workplace. Read More Global Housing Technology Challenge (GHTC) - India MoHUA has initiated the Global Housing Technology Challenge - India (GHTC-India) which aims to identify and mainstream a basket of innovative construction technologies from across the globe for housing construction sector that are sustainable, eco-friendly and disaster-resilient. Light House Projects Enrol as T ECHNOGRAHI Read More CLSS Awas Portal (CLAP) A web based monitoring system, CLSS Awas Portal (CLAP) is a common platform where all stakeholders i.e. MoHUA, Central Nodal Agencies, Primary Lending Institutions, Beneficiaries and Citizens are integrated in real time environment. The portal facilitates processing of applications along with tracking of subsidy status by beneficiaries. CLSS tracker has also been incorporated in PMAY(U) mobile App and UMANG platform. pages.text: PMAY (U) Government of India Custom Search | | Sort byRelevanceDate | Apply for PMAY-U 2.0 Angikaar 2026 Guidelines Compendium of Good Practices PMAY-U Newsletters PMAY-U Dashboard Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme CLSS Toll-Free Helpline Numbers NHB: 1800-11-3377, 1800-11-3388 HUDCO: 1800-11-6163 SBI : 1800-11-2018 The Mission, in order to expand institutional credit flow to the housing needs of urban poor will implement credit linked subsidy component as a demand side intervention. Interest subsidy will be credited upfront to the loan account of beneficiaries through Primary Lending Institutions resulting in reduced effective housing loan and Equated Monthly Instalment (EMI). The Net Present Value (NPV) of the interest subsidy will be calculated at a discount rate of 9 %. Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme for EWS/LIG Beneficiaries of Economically Weaker section (EWS) and Low Income Group (LIG) seeking housing loans from Banks, Housing Finance Companies and other such institutions would be eligible for an interest subsidy at the rate of 6.5% for a tenure of 20* years or during tenure of loan whichever is lower. The credit linked subsidy will be available only for loan amounts upto Rs 6 lakhs and additional loans beyond Rs. 6 lakhs, if any, will be at nonsubsidized rate. Credit linked subsidy would be available for housing loans availed for new construction and addition of rooms, kitchen, toilet etc. to existing dwellings as incremental housing. The carpet area of houses being constructed under this component of the mission should be upto 30 square metres and 60 square metres for EWS and LIG, respectively in order to avail of this credit linked subsidy. The beneficiary, at his/her discretion, can build a house of larger area but interest subvention would be limited to first Rs. 6 lakh only. Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme for MIG* The CLSS for MIG covers two income segments in the MIG viz. Rs.6,00,001 to Rs.12,00,000 (MIG-I) and Rs.12,00,001 to Rs.18,00,000 (MIG-II) per annum. In the MIG-I, an interest subsidy of 4% has been provided for loan amounts up to Rs.9 lakh while in MIG-II, an interest subsidy of 3% has been provided for loan amount of Rs.12 lakh. The interest subsidy will be calculated at 9% NPV over a maximum loan tenure of 20 years or the actual tenure, whichever is lesser. Housing loans above Rs. 9 lakh and Rs. 12 lakh will be at non-subsidized rates. CLSS for MIG will support acquisition/ construction of house (including re-purchase) of 160 square meters and 200 square meters carpet area as per income eligibility National Housing Bank (NHB), Housing and Urban Development Corporation (HUDCO) and State Bank of India(SBI) have been identified as Central Nodal Agencies (CNAs) to channelize this subsidy to the lending institutions and for monitoring the progress of this component. Ministry may notify other institutions as CNA in future. Under the Mission, beneficiaries can take advantage under one component only. For details, please refer to website section on Guidelines. *with effect from 01.01.2017 CLSS Subsidy Calculator : https://pmayuclap.gov.in/content/html/Subsidy-Calc.html List of PLIs who have signed MoUs with Central Nodal Agencies (CNAs) i) CLSS for EWS/LIG (as on 31.12.2017) ii) CLSS for MIG (as on 31.12.2017) Amendments in guidelines of Pradhan Mantri Awas Yajana (Urban) Mission- regarding Amendments in guidelines of Pradhan Mantri Awas Yajana (Urban) Mission- regarding Increase in the carpet area of houses eligible for interest subsidy under the Credit link Subsidy Scheme (CLSS) for the Middle Income Group (MIG) under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Urban) June-2018 Increase in the carpet area of houses eligible for interest subsidy under the Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS) for the Middle Income Group (MIG) under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Urban). pages.text: PMAY (U) Government of India Custom Search | | Sort byRelevanceDate | Apply for PMAY-U 2.0 Angikaar 2026 Guidelines Compendium of Good Practices PMAY-U Newsletters PMAY-U Dashboard PMAY (Urban) Mobile Application The PMAY (Urban) mobile application was launched on 14th Feb, 2019 by the Hon'ble Minister of State (I/C) for Housing and Urban Affairs to bring PMAY (Urban) beneficiaries directly in contact with the Mission. The application allows registered beneficiaries of PMAY (U) to upload photos/selfies of house and video of their stories/testimonies and experiences of owning a house under PMAY (U) alongwith Geo-attributes. This mobile app also allows Citizens to know the details about the scheme, about PMAY (U) progress, FAQs, Social Media Handles and latest news and updates. Need Assessment of PMAY (U) beneficiaries with respect to the Ayushman Bharat and Ujjwala schemes is also captured through angikaar module of the app by registered angikaar Resource Person (ARPs). CLSS Tracker in PMAY (U) app – Ministry is in process of adding CLSS tracker in app in order to facilitate CLSS beneficiaries to track the status of their application for CLSS Interest Subsidy on their home loan application. For effective delivery of citizen centric services, PMAY(U) app is integration on real time basis with following systems. PMAY(Urban) MIS system Ayushman Bharat MIS system Ujjwala MIS system CLSS Awas Portal(CLAP) for CLSS Tracker and CLSS Subsidy Calculator PMAY(U) website and its CMS NIC SMTP Server Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) On PMAY (U) Mobile Application Link to install PMAY (Urban) Mobile Application Home Terms & Conditions Privacy Policy Hyperlinking Policy Copyright Policy Sitemap Copyright © 2019 Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, Government of India, All rights reserved. | | |

This will provide ease of living to urban migrants/ poor in Industrial Sector as well as in non-formal urban economy to get access to dignified affordable rental housing close to their workplace. Read More Global Housing Technology Challenge (GHTC) - India MoHUA has initiated the Global Housing Technology Challenge - India (GHTC-India) which aims to identify and mainstream a basket of innovative construction technologies from across the globe for housing construction sector that are sustainable, eco-friendly and disaster-resilient. Light House Projects Enrol as T ECHNOGRAHI Read More CLSS Awas Portal (CLAP) A web based monitoring system, CLSS Awas Portal (CLAP) is a common platform where all stakeholders i.e. MoHUA, Central Nodal Agencies, Primary Lending Institutions, Beneficiaries and Citizens are integrated in real time environment. The portal facilitates processing of applications along with tracking of subsidy status by beneficiaries. CLSS tracker has also been incorporated in PMAY(U) mobile App and UMANG platform. pages.title: PMAY (U)
pages.text: PMAY (U) Government of India Custom Search | | Sort byRelevanceDate | Apply for PMAY-U 2.0 Angikaar 2026 Guidelines Compendium of Good Practices PMAY-U Newsletters PMAY-U Dashboard PMAY(URBAN) BACKGROUND In pursuance of Government’s vision of facilitating Housing for All, Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA), Government of India is implementing Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana- Urban (PMAY-U) – ‘Housing for All’ Mission since 25.06.2015. The original mission period was up to 31.03.2022 which has been extended till 31.12.2025 for completion of houses sanctioned up to 31.03.2022, without changing funding pattern and implementation methodology of the scheme. The validity of CLSS vertical was up to 31.03.2022. Under the Mission, Ministry has been providing Central assistance to States/ UTs for addressing the housing requirement of the slum dwellers and other people belonging to Economically Weaker Sections (EWS), Low Income Group (LIG) and Middle Income Group (MIG) categories in the urban areas through following four verticals: 1. Beneficiary-led Individual House Construction or Enhancement (BLC) : Under this vertical, Central Assistance of ₹1.5 lakh is provided to individual eligible families belonging to EWS categories. 2. Affordable Housing in Partnership with public or private sector (AHP): Central Assistance of ₹1.5 lakh per EWS house is provided by Government of India in projects where at least 35% of the houses in the projects are for EWS category and a single project has at least 250 houses. 3. “In-situ” Slum Redevelopment (ISSR): Slum redevelopment grant of ₹1 lakh per house is admissible for all houses built for eligible slum dwellers under the vertical of In-situ Slum Redevelopment using land as Resource with participation of private developer. 4. Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS): An interest subsidy of 6.5%, 4% and 3% on loan amount upto ₹6 lakh, ₹9 lakh and ₹12 lakh were admissible for the eligible beneficiaries belonging to Economically Weaker section (EWS)/Low Income Group (LIG), Middle Income Group (MIG)-I and Middle Income Group (MIG)-II respectively seeking housing loans from Banks, Housing Finance Companies and other such institutions. (CLSS for MIG was from 01.01.2017 to 31.03.2021) The Mission is implemented as a Centrally Sponsored Scheme (CSS) except the CLSS vertical of PMAY-U which is implemented as a Central Sector Scheme. BENEFICIARY FAMILY A beneficiary family under the Mission comprises of husband, wife, unmarried sons and/or unmarried daughters. The beneficiary family should not own a pucca house (an all-weather dwelling unit) either in his/her name or in the name of any member of his/her family in any part of India. An adult earning member (irrespective of marital status) is treated as a separate household provided that: He/she does not own a pucca (an all-weather dwelling unit) house in his/her name in any part of India. In case of a married couple, either of the spouses or both together in joint ownership will be eligible for a single house, subject to income eligibility of the household under the scheme. An eligible beneficiary can take advantage of only one of the four verticals of the Mission. Further, such person with pucca house having built-up area less than 21 sq.m may be included for enhancement of existing dwelling units upto 30 sq.m. However, if enhancement is not possible on account of lack of availability of land/space or any other reason, she/he may get a house under PMAY(U) elsewhere. SALIENT DETAILS Flexibility to States : Cafeteria Approach: States/UTs were free to choose any vertical of the Mission to meet their demand of housing Demand Based Approach: Housing requirement based on demand assessment by States/UTs Appraisal and Approval of projects at State level Ownership of House: House to be in the name of adult female member or in joint name Preference to persons with disabilities, ST/SC/OBCs, Minorities, Transgender and women (with overriding priority to widow) All houses to have drinking water, power supply, sewerage and toilet facility Coverage: All statutory towns (Census 2011) and towns notified subsequently Notified Planning Areas and Notified Development Authorities (excluding rural areas) Areas falling within notified Planning/Development area under the jurisdiction of an Industrial Development Authority/Special Area Development Authority/ Urban Development Authority or any such Authority under State Legislation. MONITORING MECHANISMS PMAY-U Dashboard The dashboard has key information related to all the physical and financial progress of the Mission. It displays project wise data of all the houses under the Mission through easy-to-understand indicators. The Dashboard has information related to State/ULB-wise sanctioned houses, the current status & the timeline of the project- execution/ occupation stages, release of Central Assistance, status of utilization certificates, project timelines, graphical view of various stages of execution, etc. Centre, State/UT/City level Login IDs have been created for all stakeholders enabling them to have day-to-day status of each project in the respective State/UT. This helps each of the Implementing Agency to identify lags in performance of the project, and rectify the issues for better implementation. Management Information System (MIS) A comprehensive and robust Management Information System (MIS) has been developed which helps all stakeholders to seamlessly manage information pertaining to physical and financial progress. The MIS allows submitting on-line demand survey with tracking facility and helps in housekeeping of various records through digitization of survey data, project information, beneficiary details, fund utilization, etc. MIS has also been integrated with CLAP for real-time monitoring mechanism, UMANG Mobile App, NITI Aayog Dashboard and DBT Bharat Portal for dissemination of information. The MIS has also been linked with the MIS of Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Gramin (PMAY-G) for verifying duplication of beneficiaries . Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) The transfer of the Central Assistance and State/ULB share to the beneficiaries of the BLC vertical of the Mission is being done by States/UTs through DBT mode where the instalments are credited directly into the beneficiary’s bank account as per the geo-tagged stage of the construction with PFMS and GIS based MIS, etc. Geo-Tagging MIS is equipped with five staged Geo-tagging features and integrated with BHUVAN Portal of National Remote Sensing Centre (NRSC) and BHARAT MAP of National Informatics Centre (NIC) for monitoring the progress of construction of houses under the BLC, ISSR and AHP verticals. Images of geo-tagged houses as moderated by States/ UTs are available in public domain. CLSS Awas Portal (CLAP) CLAP has been designed, developed and implemented to provide a transparent and robust real-time web-based monitoring system for CLSS beneficiaries. The CLAP software works in tandem on a real-time basis with the UIDAI, CNAs, PLIs and PMAY-U MIS system for Aadhaar validation and de-duplication with other verticals of PMAY-U. The component of CLSS has been closed on 31.03.2022. PMAY-U Mobile App The application is being used under PMAY-U for dissemination of information and capturing the emotions of beneficiaries through photographs/video testimonials. TECHNOLOGY SUB-MISSION (TSM) TSM was set up under PMAY-U with an objective to promote ‘Sustainable Technological Solutions for faster and cost effective construction of houses suiting to geo-climatic and hazard of the country. The Global Housing Technology Challenge- India was initiated in 2019 to identify and mainstream globally best available proven construction technologies that are sustainable, green and disaster-resilient to enable a paradigm shift in affordable housing sector. The major activities under GHTC-India are as follows: Light House Projects (LHPs) As a part of GHTC- India, six LHPs, comprising of around 1000 houses each, have demonstrated construction of ready to live houses using alternate and innovative technologies. The LHPs have served as live laboratories for different aspects of transfer of technology to the field which includes planning, design, production of components, construction practices and testing. The objective is to mainstream the globally identified proven innovative technologies under GHTC-India in Indian context. 1. LHP Chennai: Inaugurated by Hon’ble Prime Minister on 26th May 2022, this project comprises of 1,152 houses built with Precast Concrete Construction System - precast components were assembled at site. 2. LHP Rajkot : Inaugurated by Hon’ble Prime Minister on 19th October 2022, this project comprises of 1,144 houses built using ‘Monolithic Concrete Construction using Tunnel Formwork’. 3. LHP Indore : Inaugurated by Hon’ble Prime Minister on 5th October 2023, this project comprises of 1,024 houses built using ‘Prefabricated Sandwich Panel System with Pre-engineered Steel Structural System’. 4. LHP Lucknow : Inaugurated by Hon’ble Prime Minister on 10th March 2024, this project comprises of 1,040 houses built using ‘Stay-in-place PVC Formwork with Pre-Engineered Steel Structural System’. 5. LHP Ranchi : Inaugurated by Hon’ble Prime Minister on 10th March 2024, this project comprises of 1,008 houses built using ‘Precast Concrete Construction System - 3D Volumetric’ technology. 6. LHP Agartala : Comprises of 1,000 houses, this project is in advance stage of construction using ‘Light Gauge Steel Framed System with Pre-engineered Steel Structural System’ technology. TECHNOGRAHIS An online enrolment module developed for all stakeholders to register themselves and visit LHP sites for learning the use of latest innovative technologies and adaption as per their local needs. ‘Technograhis’ include faculty & students of IITs/ NITs/ Engineering colleges/ Planning & Architecture colleges, Technical Professionals engaged in Private/ Public sectors, Central/ State/ ULB officials, Start-ups/ Innovators/ Entrepreneurs, and other concerned stakeholders. Around 40,000 Technograhis were enrolled across six LHPs till 31st March 2024. Demonstration Housing Projects (DHPs) DHPs comprise of around 40 dwelling units each to facilitate wide spread dissemination and adoption of new/alternate and sustainable building materials and technologies for the large-scale replication in different geo-climatic region of the country. These DHPs will have a common dining hall with kitchen, activity room, medical room, office, shops, etc. with special features like with Rain Water Harvesting and Solar street lights and are being used for social purposes. DHPs in Ahmedabad, Agartala, Ayodhya, Bhopal, Bhubaneswar, Biharshariff, Hyderabad, Lucknow, Nellore, Panchkula, and Tirppur are complete. DHPs in Dimapur, Guwahati and Jammu are in advanced stages of construction. RACHNA Resilient Affordable Comfortable Housing through National Action (RACHNA) is a series of training programs on Innovative Construction Technologies & Thermal Comfort for Affordable Housing. A total of 75 region wise training and workshops were conducted under RACHNA. The focus of these trainings was on creating awareness among stakeholders on thermal comfort, its necessity in the affordable housing sector and ways to achieve it. More than 11,000 participants from Government/ Private sector, Academia, Masons/Artisans etc. participated in RACHNA Trainings. NAVARITIH New, Affordable, Validated, Research Innovation Technologies for Indian Housing (NAVARITIH) is a certificate course on Innovative Construction Technologies in collaboration with SPA Delhi and BMTPC. The course has familiarized the professionals with the latest materials and technologies being used worldwide for housing and has created awareness of the state-of-the-art materials/ technologies in terms of properties, specifications, performance, design and construction methodologies. A total of 1,123 participants in 17 batches have successfully completed the course till 31st March 2024. E-Learning sessions & webcasting of LHPs To disseminate knowledge about the technology, construction process, sustainability, and mass-cum-fast construction to Technograhis. These webinars / training sessions are targeted for Faculty and students, technical professionals, start-ups/ entrepreneurs/ innovators who have enrolled themselves as “Technograhis”. A total of 12 webcasting sessions at LHPs sites have been conducted which was attended by more than 6,500 stakeholders virtually/physically. Exhibition & Conferences In order to widen the horizon and to bring technology transition in the construction sector for sustainable development, various exhibition-cum-conferences were organized to bring together all global and indigenous innovative technologies, materials and process shortlisted under GHTC-India and showcase them to the relevant stakeholders on a common platform. Construction Technology India - 2019 Inaugurated by Hon'ble Prime Minister, the Expo-Cum Conference was organized on 2 -3 March, 2019 in New Delhi. The expo brought together multiple stakeholders involved in innovative and alternative housing technologies, for exchange of knowledge and business opportunities through an exhibition, thematic sessions, panel discussions and master classes. Indian Housing Technology Mela - 2021 IHTM was organised on 5-7 October 2021 in Lucknow. The expo aimed to bring together innovative indigenous construction technology and material providers, innovators, entrepreneurs involved in low and mid-rise housing (upto four storeys) and end users at a common platform to achieve affordability, sustainability, disaster resilience while facilitating faster construction. Indian Urban Housing Conclave - 2022 Inaugurated by Hon'ble Prime Minister, the conclave was organised on 19-21 October 2022 in Rajkot. The conclave and exhibition brought together Global and Domestic exhibitors who showcased the prototype models on new and innovative construction technologies/ materials. Technology providers from across the globe, domestic indigenous technologies, potential future technologies (start-ups) from the country exhibited their innovative construction technologies, materials and process. Adoption of New/ Emerging Building Materials and Technologies in Construction Industry - 2023 The conference was organised on 25th August 2023 in New Delhi in association with CREDAI. It brought together real estate developers, subject matter experts, urban practitioners and stakeholders from various sectors of the construction industry for cross learning and sharing of ideas for adoption and mainstreaming of innovative construction technologies, materials and processes. ARHCs - Affordable Rental Housing Complexes For providing decent rental housing at affordable rates to urban migrants/poor near their place of work, ARHCs was initiated as a sub-scheme under PMAY-U. This will provide ease of living to urban migrants/poor in Industrial Sector as well as in non-formal urban economy to get access to dignified affordable rental housing close to their workplace. ARHC scheme is being implemented through two models: Model-1: Utilizing existing Government funded vacant houses to convert into ARHCs through Public Private Partnership or by Public Agencies. Under Model-1, a total of 5,648 existing Government funded vacant houses (Chandigarh-2,195, Surat-393, Ahmedabad-1,376, Chittorgarh-480, Rajkot-698, Dehradun-70, Lalkuan-100, & Jammu-336) have been converted into ARHCs. Proposal for converting 7,413 vacant houses into ARHC units have been processed in the States of Gujarat, Himachal Pradesh, Haryana, Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan. Model-2: Construction, Operation and Maintenance of ARHCs by Public/Private Entities on their own available vacant land. Under Model-2, proposals of 82,273 new ARHC units in 7 States approved. Work for construction of new ARHCs comprising of 44,114 units is in progress in various cities. At Sriperumbudur, Tamil Nadu, 3,969 units have been completed and are ready for inauguration. PMAY-Urban Awards In a bid to recognize the outstanding performance by States/UTs, ULBs and Beneficiaries, the PMAY(U) Awards have been instituted. The overarching purpose of the awards is to identify and felicitate the “Achievers” and encourage others to compete and attain the goal of ‘Housing for All’ in a sustained manner. I. PMAY-U Awards 2019 Hon’ble Prime Minister presented awards to Best Performing States for successful implementation of PMAY(U) and announced the awards for other categories through a video film. All the awards were presented through digital mode. The states of Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh and Andhra Pradesh were awarded 1st, 2nd and 3rd position respectively in large State Category. Tripura revived the best performing State award in NER & Hilly States category under PMAY-U. II. PMAY-U Awards 2021 (150 Days Challenge) Hon’ble Prime Minister, during Indian Urban Housing Conclave-2022 in Rajkot, Gujarat, presented awards to Best Performing States for successful implementation of the mission. PMAY-U Awards 2021 were instituted in the form of a ‘150 Day Challenge’ for promoting a healthy competition amongst States/UTs to fast-track implementation mode to ensure completion and delivery of houses to the beneficiaries on time within the Mission period. The awards of Best Performing State category were presented by Hon’ble Prime Minister to the States of Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh and Tamil Nadu as 1st, 2nd and 3rd positions respectively. Gujarat was awarded Best State in implementation of CLSS scheme Tripura was awarded with Best Performing State under North-East and Hilly Category States. III. PMAY-U Awards 2023 The third edition of PMAY-U Awards 2023 focuses on recognizing the proactive participation of beneficiaries from across the country and appreciating the commendable work done by States/UTs, ULBs, IAs under all four verticals of the Scheme i.e., BLC, CLSS, AHP and ISSR. The results of PMAY-U Awards 2023 are yet to be announced. Technology Sub-Mission will also facilitate preparation and adoption of layout designs and building plans suitable for various geo-climatic zones. It will also assist States/ Cities in deploying disaster resistant and environment friendly technologies. The Sub-mission will coordinate with various regulatory and administrative bodies for mainstreaming and up scaling the deployment of modern construction technologies and material in place of conventional construction. Technology sub-mission will also coordinate with other agencies working in green and energy efficient technologies, climate changes etc. The Sub-Mission will work on following aspects: • Design & Planning • Innovative technologies & materials • Green buildings using natural resources and • Earthquake and other disaster resistant technologies and designs. Simple concept of designs ensuring adequate sunlight and air should be adopted. Centre and State would also partner with willing IITs, NITs and Planning & Architecture institutes for developing technical solutions, capacity building and handholding of States and Cities. State or region specific needs of technologies and designs would also be supported under this Sub-Mission. A technical cell has been setup in the Building Materials and Technology Promotion Council (BMTPC) under the Ministry to support the Sub-mission. | Portals and Mobile Apps | Financial Assistance | Demonstration Housing Projects (DHPs) | Important OMs | Important SoRs | Minutes of CSMC, Special Meetings | Publications | Audio Video | | Portals and Mobile Apps | 1 | Disaster mitigation and management | | 2 | Mobile Application on Earthquake Hazard Map of India | | 3 | Draft Protocol for Testing of Structural Components and Systems - pages.title: PMAY (U)
pages.text: PMAY (U) Government of India Custom Search | | Sort byRelevanceDate | Apply for PMAY-U 2.0 Angikaar 2026 Guidelines Compendium of Good Practices PMAY-U Newsletters PMAY-U Dashboard Awas Par Samvaad SoP on Awas Par Samvaad List of Approved Workshop/Seminar Download Certificate Click here to Participate Download Certificate List of Approved Workshop/Seminar Flow Chart Overview Objective Illustrative themes Implementation Methodology Financials Registration, Sign in and Booking of Schedule Workshop Model Expected Outcomes Reference Materials Disclaimer Overview Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Urban) [PMAY-U] Mission under Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) proposes to conduct workshops and seminars 'Awas Par Samvaad' for multiple stakeholders i.e. urban practitioners, engineering/planning/architecture/social sciences/finance students, faculties, academia, Civil Society Organisations, etc., from 1st July to 30th September 2021 through Educational Institutions and Primary Lending Institutions (PLIs )/Banks/ Housing Finance Companies (HFCs) in association with States/UTs. Objective Awas Par Samvaad aims to deliberate, discuss, disseminate share learnings and knowledge on 'Housing in Urban Spaces' amongst multiple stakeholders belonging to varied streams of learning and practices e.g. engineering, urban community development, planning, finance, etc. Illustrative themes An educational institution/PLI can choose to conduct seminar/workshop on one theme only. Target Audience: Multiple stakeholders: urban practitioners, students of engineering, urban planning, town planning, architecture, social sciences, geography, other humanities subjects, finance, economics, Marketing, Micro Finance, faculties, academia, Civil Society Organisations, Real Estate Sector, Bankers, public representatives, community leaders, Government officials of other urban missions like Smart Cities, AMRUT etc and other stakeholders. | Theme | Topics for Panel Discussions | | Interventions through PMAY-U Mission | Learnings and opportunities of 'Housing for All' misison Transformational stories of Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana- Urban Vision of Urban India- 2050 Transforming Urban Landscape | | Inclusive Housing | Inclusive approach in Urban Housing Widening horizons through inclusivity – promoting healthy and liveable cities Vision for a slum free India- approaches and strategies and implementation mechanisms Role of private sector in Affordable Housing Impact of RERA –Home Buyers feel more secure under the regime of RERA Model Tenancy Act (MTA) a game changer in India upcoming rental market Policy measures by Government of India in promoting affordable housing Global innovations and best practices in affordable housing Affordable Rental Housing Complexes (ARHCs) for an Aatmanirbhar Bharat | | Technology and Innovation | New emerging construction technologies and opportunities for Technograhis to learn and adopt from Light House Projects (LHPs) with real time demonstration Changing faces of construction practices in India Role of indigenous housing technologies in transforming Indian construction sector using local material and practices Transformational role of innovative construction technologies in mass housing and large- scale adoption Methods to make housing climate resilient adopting environment friendly practices and meeting SDG goals Measures of Quality Control & Assurance in housing projects | | Financial Inclusion | Mainstreaming Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) into the formal banking sector Empowering young professionals and Middle Income Group (MIG) to own a house through CLSS Role of Banks, HFCs and Micro-Finance Institutions (MFIs) in facilitating access to finance for urban poor in all verticals of PMAY(U) Contribution of CLSS in reaching out to all sections of society for achieving the goal of Housing for All Creating conducive housing eco-system in order to enable to facilitate stakeholder participation such as builders-bankers-beneficiaries (BBB) at City level Strategies to facilitate financial mechanisms in order to address beneficiary gap funding for completion of PMAY (U) homes | | Social Mobilisation | Significance of social mobilization and community engagement in social housing projects including adoption and acceptance of new environment Transformational changes in the life of PMAY (U) beneficiary Ways to promote cohesive living in group housing projects and managing social infrastructure and community services Methods to integrate livelihood opportunities with group housing projects Role of social workers as change agents in addressing issues through advocacy ensuring satisfactory delivery of services Relevance of social audit in housing programmes and ways to make it effective | Implementation Methodology The Mission proposes to initially conduct 75 seminars and workshops across States/UTs through the following schedule: Registration through PMAY (U) website from 1st to 20th July 2021. Seminars/Workshops: 21st July to 30th September 2021. Seminars/workshops can be conducted: OFFLINE- classroom model (WEBCAST compulsory) If OFFLINE, four sessions of 30-40 mins each, including a 10 minute session to showcase PMAY (U) videos and life transformation stories of PMAY(U) Beneficiaries. ONLINE ONLY- If ONLINE, a session for ONE hour should be conducted, including a 10 minute session to showcase PMAY (U) videos and life transformation stories of PMAY(U) Beneficiaries. For the successful roll out of 75 seminars/workshops, MoHUA, has developed a 'Awas Par Samvaad' module in the PMAY (U) website: https://pmay-urban.gov.in A helpdesk- Phone Nos. 011- 23061827 , 23063285 , to facilitate educational institutions/PLIs is made available at MoHUA. Educational institutions/ PLIs may write to kka-pmayurban@gov.in for any queries. While broad themes have been suggested by MoHUA, institutions will have the leverage to identify topics for panel discussions. However, it is mandated that every workshop should include a session on PMAY(U) mission, life transformation journey - experience sharing , show casing videos and short lms of PMAY(U). MoHUA in association with UN-Habitat will issue a certicate of participation which can be downloaded from the PMAY(U) website https://pmay-urban.gov.in The workshop/seminar shall not exceed the strength of maximum 50 participants (in Classroom model, i.e. OFFLINE) per workshop/seminar, adhering to all COVID-19 safety protocols. The State Level Nodal Agency shall be the coordinating agency. It will also approve and recommend the educational institutions for onward review and approval by MoHUA. The Commissionerm or Executive Ocer (EO) of ULB shall nominate respective City Level Technical Cell (CLTC) specialists to orient and train beneficiaries to articulate their experiences during the workshop. The ULB Commissioner/ EO and the respective CLTC specialists shall oversee the workshop proceedings and provide all necessary facilitation for the institutions and PLIs. Financials MoHUA has provisioned an amount of Rs 35,000/- for each seminar/workshop conducted shall be provided as reimbursement to Institutions. Break-up of which is as per the table below: With respect to ONLINE ONLY seminars/workshops an amount of Rs 7,000/- per workshop/seminar, has been provided catering to the Honorarium of panelists (1 session, minimum of 3 panelists) and Rs 1000/- towards expenditure for banners. Further, no cost will be provided for life transformation journey- experience sharing by PMAY (U) beneficiaries, in ONLINE ONLY workshops/seminars as beneficiaries will be interacting from their residence only. The expenditure for reimbursement shall be claimed in a prescribed format certied by the Head of the Department and Nodal Ofcer for the seminar/ workshop and submitted to the SLNA along with all event reports, photographs, videos, media coverage, and detailed list of participants for reimbursement and uploading the same on the PMAY(U) website. The SLNA will meet this expenditure from the State IEC budget under Capacity Building. Registration, Sign in and Booking of Schedule Educational Institutions/Primary Lending Institutions (PLIs) shall visit PMAY (Urban) website https://pmay-urban.gov.in and register themselves along with nodal officer details. After successful registration, educational institutions/ PLIs will login based on mobile number and OTP validation and submit a proposal to conduct workshop. Educational institutions/ PLIs will submit the information against the parameters mentioned below:- Themes and topics for discussion Objective of the Workshop/seminar Expected outcome Details of panelists and participants Agenda of the workshop/seminar Copy of proposal Name and details of the Nodal Officer for Workshop/Seminar After submission of the proposal by educational institutions/ PLIs, State/UT will download and evaluate the proposal. During evaluation, State/UT can either: Reject Accept Raise a query to Institutes/PLIs If State/UT rejects a proposal, it will mention the reason for rejection and the system will display the status to the Educational Institutions/ PLIs. If State/UT raises a query, then State/UT will post the query and request Educational Institutions/ PLIs to submit explanation for the same. If State/UT accepts, it will forward the proposal to MoHUA along with remarks and recommendation. MoHUA will download and evaluate proposal. During evaluation, MoHUA can either Reject Accept If MoHUA rejects the proposal, it will mention the reason for rejection and system will display status to the State/UT and educational institutions/ PLIs. After MoHUA approves the proposal, the system will display result inside State/UT and educational institutions/ PLIs login and send SMS alert to the concerned stakeholder. After Seminar/Workshop, Institutes/PLIs will upload following:- Proceeding and list of attendees Photos Videos YouTube link Dossier on Print and Social Media Coverage Upload signed copy of UC to the SLNA. Logo of Educational Institutions/Primary Lending to be included in the certificate After submission of proceedings by Educational Institutions/ PLIs, system will generate participant wise Certicate of Participation and send SMS to the participants to download certicate from the PMAY (U) website https://pmay-urban.gov.in after entering registered mobile number and OTP validation. Educational insititution/PLIs will upload the certied copy of the UC for reimbursement, along with all event reports, photographs, videos, media coverage, and detailed list of participants. The SLNA will verify and forward the same to MoHUA for reimbursement. The registration of workshops/ seminars are limited to 75 in number for States/UTs. MoHUA solely reserves the right to amend or permit or revise the number of workshops/seminars in States/UTs on its own discretion. Workshop Model The workshop shall be conducted for around three to four hours factoring all panel discussions, sessions, show casing lms, dialogues, experience sharing, including Q&A. Panel discussions (minimum 3 members per panel) and sessions shall consist of lectures by eminent speakers, life transformation journey - experience sharing by PMAY (U) beneficiaries, showcasing videos and short lms of PMAY (U). Institutions shall coordinate with the concerned ULBs for mobilizing PMAY (U) beneficiaries to be a part of the workshop/seminar. Institutions shall share the proceedings of the workshop through YouTube and upload photographs on PMAY (U) website. Highlights and key lessons/actions from each workshop shall be reported in at least one blog post or press release in local media and on PMAY (U) website. They shall propagate the programme actively through press, respective website, social media such as Facebook, Twitter, urge students and stakeholders share details of seminars/ workshops through their individual/Organisational accounts. Expected Outcomes 75 seminars/workshops will be conducted across the Nation. 'Housing for All' Mission would have promoted housing as a subject with more practical and relevant experiences from the grassroot. A platform for student, professionals and urban practitioners to enable them learn about government policies/frameworks for future application and implementation of housing programmes. The seminars/workshops will trigger young minds to explore the realms of large scale government programmes and further bring in new perspectives in their dealings as professionals. The seminars/workshops would set a precedent for educational institutions to conduct more programmes as a part of their curriculum, addressing the universality of housing from various perspectives. The events are expected to create a buzz around housing among various stakeholders by wide media coverage of events by institutions. Surge in activities on social media by students and institutions through information sharing among peer groups. Reference Materials | Open File of Awas Par Samvaad Logo | View | | Interventions through PMAY-U Mission | View | | Inclusive Housing | View | | Technology and Innovation | View | | Financial Inclusion | View | | Social Mobilisation | View | | Videos for All Themes | View | Disclaimer MoHUA reserves the right to use photographs and videos of the seminars/workshops in any appropriate platforms for the purpose of dissemination. With respect to Transformational stories of PMAY (U) beneficiaries, it is assumed that the educational institution has taken the consent of the beneficiary for use of her/ his photograph and video. pages.title: PMAY (U)
pages.text: PMAY (U) Government of India Custom Search | | Sort byRelevanceDate | Apply for PMAY-U 2.0 Angikaar 2026 Guidelines Compendium of Good Practices PMAY-U Newsletters PMAY-U Dashboard Khushiyon Ka Aashiyana - Short Film Contest 2021 SoP on Short Film Contest Click here to Participate Flow Chart Introduction Implementation Mechanism Certificate of Participation Certificate & Cash Prize for Winners Contestant's role and necessary steps to be taken Roles & Responsibilities Technical Specification of the Short Film Illustrative Theme for Short Film Scrutiny of the video film entry Important Dates Selection of Winners and Notification Disclaimer Introduction Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Urban (PMAY-U), a flagship Mission of Government of India is being implemented by Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA). PMAY (U) is organising a Short Film Contest 2021 - "Khushiyon Ka Aashiyana". Implementation Mechanism Short Films are invited from PMAY (U) beneficiaries, students, youth, civil society organisations, institutions and individual/groups on a broad theme of "transforming human lives in urban landscape through PMAY (U) during it six years of journey". The contest will be open to all Citizens/Indian Nationals above the age of 18 years except to employees and associates of MoHUA/ Urban Development Department of the State/ Urban Local Body (ULB) regardless of where they live. States/UTs/ ULBs, SLTC/ CLTC specialists will facilitate the contestants in identifying & selecting PMAY (U) beneficiaries and in shooting of the short film. The language of the film shall be in English or Hindi or any vernacular language. Period of Production: Films produced during the contest period 1st July, 2021 at 10:00 a.m. IST to 30th September, 2021, at 5:00 p.m. only will be accepted. Entry limits: Each participant will be allowed to submit only one entry in this contest. If more than one entry is submitted, the entry received first alone will be considered. If a participant/contestant submits more than one entry in a single cover, the application will be rejected. Certificate of Participation Certificate of participation will be available for download after successful moderation and vetting by States/UTs. The system will send SMS to all contestants to download certificate of participation from the website: https://pmay-urban.gov.in through their registered mobile numbers. Certificate & Cash Prize for Winners Videos moderated and vetted by States/UTs will be forwarded to MoHUA for scrutiny and evaluation for announcement of winners. Award winners will be intimated through SMS and via email to download their certificate and upload bank related details and other required documents on the website for transfer of cash prize. | Ist Prize(25 Nos): | Rs 25,000/- | + | Certificate of Recognition | | IInd prize(25 Nos): | Rs 20,000/- | + | Certificate of Recognition | | IIIrd prize (25 Nos): | Rs 12,500/- | + | Certificate of Recognition | Contestant's role and necessary steps to be taken The Contestant shall register on the PMAY (U) mobile application. Contestant should approach the ULB and contact the respective CLTC specialist for identifying beneficiaries from the list provided in the mobile application. Contestant shall not approach PMAY (U) beneficiaries directly. In the event that there is no CLTC specialist in the desired city, the contestant shall contact the CLTC specialist of the nearby/closest city and the CLTC specialist is bound to facilitate the contestant to shoot the film. Contestant should fully and unconditionally be bound to the terms and conditions of the contest. Roles & Responsibilities Officials of States/UTs/Urban Local Bodies(ULBs), State Level Technical Cell (SLTC) /City Level Technical Cell (CLTC) specialists. The State Level Nodal Agency (SLNA) shall nominate SLTC specialists to ensure all facilitation, coordination and communication with Urban Local Bodies and CLTC specialists. SLNA, shall nominate a Nodal Officer for supervising and ensuring moderation of videos uploaded by contestants at the State level. The concerned CLTC specialist should identify, shortlist a minimum of 20 PMAY (U) beneficiaries, whose houses are complete and occupied. She/he should ensure that the house is well maintained, presentable with PMAY (U) logo. If there is no CLTC specialist in the contestant’s chosen city, the CLTC specialist of the nearby/closest city at is bound to facilitate the contestant to shoot the film. CLTC specialists shall thoroughly orient, brief and prepare beneficiaries for the film shoot. CLTC specialists should accompany the contestant to the beneficiary's house and be present during the complete process of shooting. The activity does not include any financial or logistic implication. Technical Specification of the Short Film The length of the video file should be a maximum of 3 minutes. File size for uploading video should not be more than 400MB. Recommended resolution of video is 1080 x 1920. Film can be shot on smartphone or a video camera. The video file format should be MOV or MP4. PMAY(U) beneficiaries, students, youth, non-governmental organisations, institutions and individual/groups will be able to submit nomination through following modes:- a. PMAY(Urban) Website – https://pmay-urban.gov.in b. PMAY(Urban) Mobile Application - Google play Store - https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.pmay.app_s&hl=en Apple App Store - https://apps.apple.com/in/app/pmay-urban/id1454478722 Contestants are requested to read Process Flow Chart related to registration and submission of entries of short lm attached at Annexure-A. Click here to read Process Flow Chart related to submission of entries Illustrative Theme for Short Film PMAY Urban housing for ensuring dignity of women. Dignity through PMAY(U) House. Livelihood opportunities for beneficiaries from PMAY(U). On path to self reliance and rediscovering the pride of owning a house. Improved quality of life through better facilities. Improved social status in the society for PMAY(U) Beneficiaries Feeling of happiness, pride and self esteem on owning a house. Improved health and performances in education for children due to better living condition. Greater sense of safety and security after owning all weather pucca house. Unique experience of beneficiary and children living in all weather pucca house. Life changing experiences of women beneficiaries after owning a house like reduced drudgery etc. A home of my own- A woman's journey/story/experience. Any other theme relevant to PMAY(U). Scrutiny of the video film entry The video files received during the Contest Period (1st July, 2021, at 10:00 a.m. IST to 10th August, 2021, at 5:00 p.m. IST) and found otherwise in order (with duly filled in prescribed Registration Form) will be scrutinized on 20th September, 2021, by the State Nodal Officer. If the video file submitted by any contestant is found to be in poor resolution the same will be informed to othe contestant from 20th-25th September 2021. They will be permitted to resubmit their video file on or before 5:00 pm on 28th September, 2021, failing which they will be disqualified from the Contest. The video files re-submitted, after 5:00 pm on 28th September, 2021 will not be accepted and will be rejected without providing any reason whatsoever. No new applications will be accepted during this period. Important dates | Start Date: | 1st July, 2021 at 10:00 AM | | Last Date of Submission: | 30th September, 2021 at 5:00 PM | | Declaration of results: | 15th October, 2021 at 5:00 PM | For queries please email in the ID - kka-pmayu@gov.in Helpdesk- Phone Nos. 011- 23061827, 23063285 Selection of Winners and Notification The entries will be evaluated by a panel of judges (Jury) at MoHUA. The Jury's decision will be final and binding and no correspondence or discussion will be entered into regarding the judging process or any of the outcomes reached. Winners will be notified by email and/or telephone. The mission shall have no liability for the Winner's failure to receive notices due to spam, junk e-mail or other security settings or due to them providing with incorrect or otherwise non-functioning contact information. Disclaimer Contestant hereby declares that participant has taken the consent of PMAY (U) beneficiary and all the characters before shooting the video. MoHUA reserves its right and discretion to select or reject any video send by participants, as per its selection criteria. MoHUA on its own discretion may alter the number of themes, prizes for the award process, if required, without stating out the reasons thereof. Any politically inclined or offensive content / violation of community standards will not be entertained and will be treated as an offense under Cyber Law. PMAY(U) Mission will not be held responsible, if the participants are not able to upload their entries on PMAY (U) website before the last date & time of submission for any reason whatsoever. Contestant understands and hereby gives her/his consent that the video uploaded on MoHUA's portal, may be used by MoHUA for any o cial purpose, however only selected videos will be awarded. No substitution of prize or transfer/assignment of prize to others or request for the cash equivalent by Winner is permitted. Contestant declares that the video is made by her/him and she/he has not used any other's audio or video in said uploaded video. No inappropriate material will be allowed. Contestant hereby indemni es MoHUA and its o cials against any claim(s) in relation with the video sent/uploaded by Participant on MoHUA's portal under this contest. Contestant (s) represent(s) and warrant(s) that he/she will comply with all the applicable Indian laws. Contestant (s) shall not disclose and/or use any information, if doing so is in violation of any obligation against confidentiality. documents.extracted_text:
May 2026
 2

 1
Introduction
1.1. Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Urban (PMAY-U), being implemented by the Ministry of Housing and Urban 
Affairs (MoHUA) since 2015, is one of the largest affordable housing schemes of the world. In the last 
decade, the Scheme has fulfilled the housing dreams of crores of beneficiaries. Under the scheme, more 
than 1.22 crore houses have been sanctioned, while around 97 lakh pucca houses, with all basic ameni-
ties, have been completed/delivered to the beneficiaries.  
1.2. Further, to support the housing needs of additional 1 crore families of urban India, PMAY-U 2.0 - ‘Housing 
for All’ Mission was launched in September 2024. PMAY-U 2.0 is being implemented through four verti -
cals i.e., Beneficiary Led Construction (BLC), Affordable Housing in Partnership (AHP), Affordable Rental 
Housing (ARH) and Interest Subsidy Scheme (ISS). Till 31st January 2026, more than 10 lakh houses have 
been sanctioned under PMAY-U 2.0. 
1.3. To raise public awareness about the scheme, encourage participation of eligible beneficiaries to avail its 
benefits and to accelerate completion and occupancy of PMAY-U houses, “Angikaar 2025” , a last-mile 
outreach campaign was launched by the Ministry on 4th September 2025. The campaign ran for a period 
of five months, till 31st January 2026. 
1.4. The Angikaar 2025 campaign witnessed an overwhelming response across India through large-scale out-
reach activities and effectively connected with people on the ground. The campaign accelerated the com-
pletion of more than 2 lakh houses under PMAY-U, grounding of more than 2.2 lakh houses under PMAY-U 
2.0. Apart from this, occupancy of AHP houses was also fast-tracked during the campaign period.  
1.5. Building upon the achievements of Angikaar 2025, the Ministry is launching Angikaar 2026 as a focused 
national outreach campaign from 4th May  2026 up to 31st July 2026. 
 2
Aims and Objectives
Coverage
Mediums of Dissemination 
The Angikaar 2026 campaign aims to strengthen last-mile citizen outreach, facilitate submission of eligible ap-
plications and accelerate the grounding, completion and occupancy of houses. It will promote geo-tagging, en-
hance convergence with allied welfare schemes, and improve on-ground monitoring and implementation.  
The key objectives of the campaign are: 
i. Fast-track completion of 11 Lakh BLC houses under PMAY-U 
ii. Ensure completion, geo-tagging and occupancy of over 3 lakh under-construction AHP houses 
iii. To accelerate the geo-tagging, grounding and completion of sanctioned PMAY-U 2.0 houses  
iv. Extend benefits of PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana and other eligible welfare schemes to PMAY-U beneficia-
ries 
v. Promote interaction and experience sharing with PMAY-U beneficiaries 
vi. Organize Grih Pravesh ceremonies for newly completed houses 
vii. Generate awareness about PMAY-U 2.0, including rental housing initiatives 
viii. Reach out to vulnerable sections of society and Special Focus Groups for wider coverage of the scheme
ix. Facilitate home loans for beneficiaries through Bank/HFC linkages, with credit guarantee coverage under 
CRGFTLIH 
x. Promote the use of innovative construction technologies, thermal comfort measures and green/sustainable 
processes in housing construction  
Angikaar 2026 would be held in all cities, ULBs and District Headquarters where PMAY-U and PMAY-U 2.0 are 
being implemented. Special emphasis shall be placed on cities with a high number of incomplete or under-con-
struction houses, districts with pending applications, and areas with a higher concentration of vulnerable and 
Special Focus Group. 
Activities under Angikaar 2026 shall be conducted at city, ward levels, ensuring last-mile outreach through 
camps, IEC activities, beneficiary facilitation events and coordination with financial institutions and line depart-
ments. 
A multi-pronged Information, Education and Communication (IEC) strategy shall be adopted to ensure wide -
spread awareness, last-mile outreach, and effective beneficiary facilitation under the Angikaar 2026 campaign. 
 3
A. Financial Facilitation & Beneficiary Support 
B. Community Outreach & Interpersonal Communication 
C. Outdoor Publicity & Physical Visibility 
i. Loan Melas: Organising loan melas in coordination with Banks and Housing Finance Companies (HFCs) to 
facilitate on-the-spot loan processing and approvals for PMAY-U 2.0 beneficiaries as well as existing PMAY-U 
beneficiaries. These melas will support beneficiaries in completing house construction, understanding 
credit options and availing credit guarantee under CRGFTLIH.
ii. Information Kiosks / Help Desks: Setting up information kiosks/booths at strategic locations to:  
• Disseminate information on eligibility, benefits, and application procedures 
• Distribute IEC material in English, Hindi, and local languages 
• Facilitate on-spot grievance redressal, application support, eligibility verification, and tracking of fund 
disbursement status 
i. Street Plays / Nukkad Nataks / Flash Mobs: Engaging local cultural and drama groups to perform street 
plays and flash mobs depicting the transformational impact of PMAY-U, eligibility criteria, and application 
processes in an engaging and relatable manner. 
ii. Door-to-Door Campaigns: Conducting door-to-door outreach through CLTCs, SHG members and commu-
nity volunteers to identify incomplete or non-starter houses, assess reasons for delays, Provide on-the-spot 
facilitation and handholding support.  
iii. Involvement of Elected Representatives/ Local Influencers: Engaging local elected representatives, com-
munity leaders, Anganwadi workers, schoolteachers, SHG leaders, beneficiaries and social influencers to 
share experiences and benefits of the scheme for enhanced credibility and wider community acceptance.  
i. Wall Paintings: Displaying wall paintings in local languages near municipality offices, ration shops, promi -
nent public places and high-footfall areas to create sustained visibility and awareness.
ii. Hoardings/Posters/Banners: Installing hoardings, posters and banners at bus stands, railway stations, 
marketplaces and government offices, prominently displaying QR codes linked to scheme information and 
online application portals for wider dissemination. 
iii. Pasting of QR Codes: Pasting QR codes linked to the Unified Web Portal (UWP) of PMAY-U 2.0 for easy ac -
cess to information and application services at beneficiaries’ houses and prominent public locations. 
iv. Mobile Vans / Roadshows: Vans equipped with posters, audio-visual content, and live demonstrations tour-
ing neighborhoods and urban areas. 
v. Community Radio Broadcasts: Broadcast of awareness programs, interviews, and FAQs via community ra-
dio stations in urban areas. 
vi. Public Transport Branding: Display campaign posters/QR codes inside buses, auto-rickshaws, metro trains, 
and taxis to reach commuters. 
vii. Interactive Touch Screens / Digital Kiosks: Install at municipal offices, bus stations, and malls to allow 
citizens to explore PMAY-U 2.0 benefits, check eligibility, and submit applications digitally. 
 4
D. Mass Media & Digital Outreach 
E. Engagement & Awareness Activities 
i. Local TV / Cable Announcements: Broadcasting announcements, short videos and success stories through 
local TV channels and cable networks to ensure maximum outreach in urban and peri-urban areas.
ii. Social media & Digital Platforms:  Leveraging digital platforms such as Facebook, X (Twitter), Instagram, 
official websites, and web banners for awareness creation, information dissemination, and promotion of 
success stories, campaigns, and key milestones. 
iii. WhatsApp / Messaging Campaigns: Use bulk WhatsApp messages and community groups to send updates, 
application reminders, and success stories. 
iv. SMS Alerts / IVRS Calls:  Inform beneficiaries about scheme updates, loan disbursements, Grih Pravesh 
events, or eligibility checks. 
v. YouTube / Short Video Campaigns: Promote short videos highlighting benefits, construction innovations, 
and success stories of PMAY-U houses.
i. Quiz Competitions: Organising quiz competitions on PMAY-U and PMAY-U 2.0 at community, school, and 
institutional levels to promote awareness and participation, especially among youth and community volun-
teers. 
ii. Urban Ward Meetings: Conduct interactive awareness sessions in urban wards or municipal areas, engag-
ing elected representatives and local officials.
iii. Beneficiary Testimonial Sessions:  Invite successful PMAY-U beneficiaries to share their experiences at 
community gatherings, schools and public meetings. 
iv. School & College Outreach:  Conduct awareness programs in schools/colleges; engage students in essay 
competitions, poster-making, or digital campaigns to reach families indirectly. 
v. Self-Help Group (SHG) Workshops: Organize workshops with SHGs for women-led households to discuss 
benefits, loan facilitation, and eligibility verification. 
vi. Folk Music & Puppetry Shows: Use local folk art, puppetry, or street concerts to convey information.
vii. Festivals & Fairs Integration: Set up PMAY-U booths, interactive games, or quiz corners at local fairs, mela, 
and festivals. 
viii. Community Photo Exhibitions: Display before-and-after pictures of completed houses to highlight impact 
and inspire participation. 
ix. Collaboration with NGOs & CBOs: Engage non-governmental and community-based organizations to reach 
vulnerable groups. 
 5
PM Awas Mela – Shehri 
i. “PM Awas Mela – Shehri” , the anchor event of Angikaar 2025, was held across District Headquarters in dif-
ferent States/UTs. This flagship event served as a vibrant platform for delivering and showcasing the benefits 
of PMAY-U and PMAY-U 2.0 while promoting convergence and community outreach at the grassroots level.  
ii. On similar lines, the PM Awas Mela – Shehri under Angikaar 2026 shall be organised on monthly basis across 
District Headquarters and ULBs in different States/UTs.  
iii. In case of larger cities, the PM Awas Mela-Shehri would be organised at the Municipal Corporation level. 
For Municipal Corporations with a population of more than 20 lakh, 1 PM Awas Mela – Shehri per 10 lakh 
population is to be organised. However, State/UT Governments may decide exceptions for bigger cities, as 
deemed necessary.  
iv. PM Awas Mela – Shehri will feature a wide array of services and engagements designed to benefit citizens, 
especially the prospective beneficiaries of PMAY-U 2.0. The key components are: 
• Help desks and information kiosks to provide guidance on eligibility, application processes and scheme 
benefits. 
• On-spot verification and registration services. 
• PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana camp to provide scheme benefits to existing PMAY-U beneficiaries.  
• Loan Melas for facilitation of home loans to provide ease of finance to beneficiaries of PMAY-U, PMAY-U 
2.0 and PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana. 
• PM SVANidhi camps, Ayushman Bharat – Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY) camps, among 
others for convergence of schemes.  
• At least three dedicated camps will be set up for the Special Focus Group beneficiaries of PMAY-U 2.0.  
• Enrolment of potential beneficiaries through details ascertained during the camps. 
• Sharing of experiences of PMAY-U beneficiaries through personal stories.  
• Wide media coverage of all activities shall be conducted during the event.
v. To ensure high footfall and active participation from all sections of society, the PM Awas Mela – Shehri will 
also include vibrant community engagement activities and cultural events, nukkad nataks, quiz competi -
tions, selfie booths, recreational activities, food stalls by PM SVANidhi beneficiaries and dedicated stalls of 
notable Self-Help Groups (SHGs).  
vi. In addition to the PM Awas Mela – Shehri, a series of events/activities will be conducted by ULBs at ward/
cluster/city level to create momentum on the Angikaar 2026 campaign through various mediums. The ac -
tivities shall focus on mobilizing the beneficiaries for timely completion of PMAY-U houses and maximum 
enrolment of potential beneficiaries under PMAY-U 2.0. 
vii. The PM Awas Mela – Shehri is expected to act as a catalyst for enhancing awareness about PMAY-U 2.0 and 
accelerating the momentum of implementation of PMAY-U 2.0. It will also bring government services closer 
to the people in an inclusive and participatory approach.  
 6
Roles & Responsibilities
State/UT Governments
i. Coordinate with Divisional Commissioners, District Magistrates, Municipal Commissioners and elected 
representatives. 
ii. Develop city/state-level IEC plans for campaign rollout. 
iii. Nominate Nodal Officers at State/UT and City/ULB levels; assign responsibilities to SLTC/CLTC 
iv. Organize training and orientation for officers deployed in the campaign. 
Implementation Methodology 
I. Angikaar 2026 will be implemented through community mobilisation and a series of IEC activities, including 
door-to-door awareness campaigns and city and ward level activities.  
II. The progress of Angikaar 2026 will be monitored by the Joint Secretary & Mission Director (HFA) and other 
officers of HFA division will assist to achieve the intended goal of the campaign.  
III. The State/UT Government Department implementing PMAY-U 2.0 Scheme would direct all the District Mag-
istrates/District Collectors and the Urban Local Body administrators to effectively plan, implement and 
monitor the activities of Angikaar 2026. The District Collectors would oversee the implementation of all the 
activities at the district level and the ULB level. The administrator of the concerned ULB would be in-charge 
of implementing Angikaar 2026 at the ULB Level.   
IV . The entire organization of this campaign at ground level would require detailed planning at the State/UT, Dis-
trict and at ULB levels for effective coordination and implementation across the country. This would require 
sharing of responsibilities at various levels, including formation of coordination committees at the district 
level, calendar dates to generate awareness and finalization of venues to host PM Awas Mela – Shehri and 
the different camps.  
V. A Coordination Committee will be formed at the district level to monitor, instruct and evaluate the imple -
mentation of the Angikaar 2026. The implementation would require community mobilisation, convergence 
with PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana and other welfare schemes, along with a series of operational and IEC 
activities.  
VI. States/UTs, Districts and ULBs are encouraged to come up with their own innovative promotional activities 
such as suitably welcome and address the citizens/beneficiaries, conduct meetings at ULB level to strate -
gize plan of action for implementation, finalization of venues for PM Awas Mela – Shehri etc. 
VII. For this campaign, the partnerships with Self Help Groups (SHGs), Civil Society Organisations (CSOs), 
elected representatives, amongst others may be leveraged by States/UTs, Districts and ULBs.  
VIII. Angikaar 2025 module, created under PMAY-U 2.0 web portal, will be suitably modified for monitoring the 
implementation of the campaign. Similar to the practice followed by States/UTs, Districts and ULBs, the 
events (data/information/photos/videos) held under Angikaar 2026, shall be uploaded on the module. The 
link to access the Angikaar 2026 portal is: https://pmaymis.gov.in/PMAYMIS2_2024/Angikaar.aspx  
Effective execution of Angikaar 2026 under PMAY-U 2.0 requires coordinated efforts across multiple stakehold-
ers at the Central, State/UT, District and ULB levels. Clearly defined roles and responsibilities are essential to 
ensure consistent monitoring, targeted outreach and community engagement. Outlined here are the key re -
sponsibilities at Central, State/UT and ULB level including that of CLTC/SLTC, field-level workers. 
 7
District level/Municipal Corporation of 20 lakh+ population
Urban Local Bodies (ULBs)
i. Constitute monitoring committees under the District Magistrate/CDO/CEO Zilla Parishad or Municipal 
Commissioner, including officials from PMAY-U 2.0, PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijili Yojana, PM SVANidhi, ULB, 
and banks. 
ii. Identify field-level staff (CLTC/SLTC) and arrange additional logistical support, security, and approvals. 
iii. Organize anchor events: finalize venue, arrange logistics, and publicity. 
iv. Map target areas, audience, and beneficiaries for outreach. 
v. Coordinate with elected representatives and ground-level staff for smooth rollout. 
vi. Conduct IEC activities for wider scheme dissemination. 
vii. Mobilize citizens for maximum registration/enrolment in demand surveys. 
viii. Select exemplary beneficiaries to share life-transforming experiences.
i. Nominate Nodal Officers and identify field-level staff. 
ii. Conduct city-level workshops for digital demand surveys (mobile/tablet/laptop). 
iii. Map target areas and audience. 
iv. Hold coordination meetings with elected representatives and ground-level staff. 
v. Execute IEC activities using multiple tools to ensure maximum participation. 
vi. Mobilize citizens for demand survey registration. 
vii. Provide logistical support, obtain permissions, and arrange security. 
viii. Update campaign information, photos, videos, and data on IT platforms in coordination with district of -
fices. 
ix. Select beneficiaries to share their success stories. 
x. Monitor progress on scheme saturation and data uploads. 
xi. Ensure overall coordination of Angikaar 2026 activities with SLTC/CLTC specialists. 
v. Liaison with parastatal departments (PR, Culture, etc.) for campaign support. 
vi. Provide guidance and logistic support to district administration. 
vii. Share timelines for campaign activities. 
viii. Monitor progress and outcomes of Angikaar 2026 within the State/UT. 
Expenditure 
Documentation 
Expenditure for the campaign will be met from the capacity building activities of PMAY-U 2.0 as per scheme guidelines. 
i. Post the completion of the Angikaar campaign, States/UTs shall prepare a post event document comprising of com-
prehensive coverage of all the activities conducted. 
ii. The Ministry shall also prepare a separate report highlighting components of the campaign. The report would include 
photographs, success stories of the beneficiaries from various Urban schemes, experience sharing of officials. 
 8
Expected Outcomes  
The Angikaar 2026 campaign under PMAY-U 2.0 aims to achieve the following outcomes: 
1. Completion of PMAY-U Houses 
• Accelerated completion of 11 lakh BLC houses under PMAY-U, ensuring beneficiaries can occupy their 
homes. 
• Ensure over 3 lakh under-construction AHP houses are completed, geo-tagged, and occupied by eligible 
beneficiaries. 
2. Accelerated geo-tagging, grounding and completion of PMAY-U 2.0 houses 
• Accelerated geo-tagging, grounding and completion of PMAY-U 2.0 houses by facilitating timely comple-
tion of beneficiary documentation, land and eligibility verification, convergence with financial institu -
tions for loan support, and coordination with ULBs 
3. Access to PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana Benefits 
• Eligible PMAY-U beneficiaries with completed houses get access to PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana and 
convergence with other welfare benefits. 
4. Provision of home loans for PMAY-U 2.0 beneficiaries  
• Facilitate home loans for 10 lakh newly included PMAY-U 2.0 beneficiaries, ensuring credit guarantee 
coverage where applicable. 
5. Resolution of pending applications under PMAY-U 2.0  
• Support applicants of PMAY-U 2.0 whose applications are pending at various stages, ensuring timely pro-
cessing and approvals. 
6. Support for Special Focus Group (SFG) 
• Targeted outreach and assistance to Special Focus Group beneficiaries identified under PMAY-U 2.0. 
7. Awareness and Inclusion of Vulnerable Sections 
• Broaden awareness and outreach to the general public and weaker/vulnerable sections of society to 
maximize participation and benefits under PMAY-U 2.0. 
8. Enhanced Beneficiary Engagement and Community Participation 
• Increased engagement with beneficiaries through IEC activities, experience-sharing sessions, and Grih 
Pravesh events, strengthening community trust and ownership of housing schemes. 
9. Adoption of innovative and sustainable housing practices 
• Promote the use of innovative construction technologies, thermal comfort measures, and green/sustain-
able processes in housing construction under PMAY-U 2.0. 
Felicitation of Achievers
The achievers and winners of various field-level events organized under Angikaar 2026 and other community 
engagement activities will be duly felicitated by the respective District Administration, ULBs and State/UT Gov -
ernments. The felicitation will serve as a recognition of their outstanding performance, active participation, and 
valuable contributions towards the success of the initiative. 
The achievements of States/UTs, Districts and ULBs during Angikaar 2025 and Angikaar 2026 will be collectively 
considered in the next edition of PMAY-U Awards hosted by MoHUA.  
 9

Joint Secretary & Mission Director (Housing for All)
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

India is Moving 
from Women Development 
to Women-Led Development



Foreword
(Durga Shanker Mishra)
Secretary, Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs   
New Delhi
08.03.2021
1
Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Urban (PMAY-U) has been transforming lives of urban 
poor by providing them all weather pucca houses with basic amenities along with 
necessary physical and social infrastructure. Scheme at its very core empowers 
women, the houses constructed or acquired are in the name of the female member 
of the household or in the joint name with lady's name at �rst. Every house 
constructed under the Scheme ensures dignity of living for the women of the 
household by providing basic civic amenities like a toilet facility, regular water 
supply, a proper kitchen with gas and electricity. I am happy to share that against 
the total assessed demand of 112 lakh houses, Ministry has already sanctioned 111 
lakh houses; out of which, 73 lakh houses are in various stages of construction and 
over 43 lakh have been delivered. 
On the occasion of International Women's Day 2021, PMAY-U is celebrating the spirit of womanhood and has brought 
out this Compendium which contains life transforming stories of the women bene�ciaries of PMAY-U who have been 
felicitated on 1st January 2021 for the most aesthetically constructed BLC houses. It has encapsulated their journey 
and emotions that come with the pride of owning their dream house. I am con�dent that these life transforming 
stories will inspire women at a large. Along with the bene�ciaries, the concerted efforts of women associated with 
the Scheme at National and States/UTs level have yielded desired results. 
As our Hon'ble Prime Minister says “the key to the house opens the door to many opportunities”, my Ministry is fully 
committed to realise his vision of 'Housing for All' by 2022. Access to better housing has dynamic inter-linking 
impacts on the lives of women, their children and the entire family. It improves their quality of lives which has 
multiple socio-economic bene�ts such as access to better health, education and livelihood, apart from social 
recognition and respect. 
During my interactions and visits to various parts of India, I have seen how our women bene�ciaries have put every 
inch of their houses to productive usage through various trades and vocations. No doubt, PMAY-U house has 
succeeded in providing wings to the entrepreneurial dreams of our women bene�ciaries. In addition to housing, my 
Ministry ensures a holistic approach to change management for the bene�ciaries through the platform of 'Angikaar'. 
I appreciate the efforts of Shri Amrit Abh�at, Mission Director, Housing for All, and all his team members, States/ UTs 
and other key stakeholders for putting in concerted efforts in ful�lling the dream of urban poor and for compiling 
success stories of award winning bene�ciaries in this Compendium. My sincere compliments to all bene�ciaries 
who have willingly come forward to share their journey and expressed experiences on owning a house of their own. 
B SARASWATHI 
ANDAMAN AND NICOBAR ISLANDS
Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Urban) [PMAY(U)] Scheme has proved to be a blessing for millions of  people and one 
such satis�ed couple is B Saraswathi and B. Veera Swamy from Port Blair, Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
B Saraswathi is a housewife. B.Veera Swamy supports his family by working as a painter. The couple is parents to a 
young son. Like everyone else, Saraswathi too dreamt of having her own house but since her husband is the only 
earning member of the family, she buried her desires and was forced to live in a rented house for years. The house 
had restrictions on usage of water and electricity. However, life moved on for them. 
Amidst this dismay, a ray of hope emerged when she heard about the PMAY(U) through the announcements made in 
her area . Saraswathi persuaded her husband to apply for the Scheme. Eventually, they received funds to construct 
the house and today, the family is a proud owner of a newly constructed house, comprising of a bedroom, hall, 
kitchen along with toilet and bathroom facilities. 
Saraswathi and Veera Swamy express their gratitude towards the Central Government for implementing PMAY(U) 
successfully and making their dream of affordable housing come true.
The home has instilled con�dence in Saraswathi and Veera Swamy's life. They are no more concerned about their 
safety and security. The home has helped them in gaining social respect in society as well.

MUNGI

Mungi Santhi (33) and her family are the role models in her community. At Nandigama Nagar Panchayat of Krishna 
District in Andhra Pradesh, the neighbours  envy them for the phenomenal life transformation the family has gone 
through, courtesy a pucca house with the assistance of Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Urban (PMAY-U).
She and her husband were conventional rag pickers and the family's traditional livelihood was piggery. They earlier 
lived in a kutcha house which did not even have a toilet in it. With the meagre income, a pucca house seemed like a 
distant dream!
Helped by acquaintances, she got enrolled under PMAY (U) and that's was the beginning of the turnaround in their life.
Once they moved into a new and pucca house, their aspirations for a better life found a concrete base. 
The family is now engaged in organised rag picking using a cycle cart.
“We have dropped piggerry and our rag picking exercise is now more hygienic,” says Mungi Santhi. That was not the 
end of story. That was, instead, the beginning of  a good life for them. “My husband has totally recovered from alcohol 
addiction  and  our livelihood has got better. My children are studying in a Government school,” she adds. 
In the community, the family's life transformation is much talked about. “They enquire with us on how we managed to 
build a new house. They congratulate us for �nding our dignity in life,” Mungi Santhi says.
